title: Farmhouse Bed — Standard King Size
summary: A planked 4x4-post farmhouse bed with a tall headboard and matching footboard, built as separate finished pieces and assembled in the bedroom.
description:
The headboard and footboard share the exact same construction — a planked 1x6 panel framed in 1x4 and capped with a 2x4 and 2x6 — with the only real difference being height and a set of cleats on the footboard's back for the side-rail brackets to notch into. Building them as identical processes, just at different heights, is what keeps a piece this large from becoming two separate design problems.

Finish the headboard, footboard, and side rails before final assembly, and bring the pieces into the bedroom to assemble rather than building it fully assembled in a shop — a king bed's footprint won't clear most doorways or stairwells once it's one piece. Mattress slats spaced about 4" apart, screwed directly into the side rails, are what's actually holding the mattress; everything above that is the look of the piece, not its structure.

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit; 1" overhang all sides.
Measure and cut to fit; 1" overhang all sides.
Measure and cut to fit; can substitute 1x4s or ripped plywood.

steps.title: Attach the headboard and footboard cleats
steps.body: Attach 2x2 cleats flush to the back of the legs with 2-1/2" screws. Pocket hole users can skip this step and attach the panel frame directly to the legs instead.

steps.title: Build the headboard panel
steps.body: Lay out the 1x6 headboard panel boards and measure the overall width. Cut 1x4 frame boards to this measurement. Attach the panel boards to the 1x4s with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails from the back side to hide nail holes, applying glue between each panel board. Check that the panel is square, not a parallelogram.

steps.title: Attach the headboard panel to the legs
steps.body: Attach the panel to the 2x2 cleats with 2-1/2" screws and glue, plus a few 1-1/4" nails through the front side into the cleat where there's no 1x4 backing.

steps.title: Cap the headboard
steps.body: Measure the top overall width and cut a 2x4 to this width. Attach with glue and screws to the top of the headboard. Cut a 2x6 2" longer than the 2x4 and attach with a 1" overhang on all sides, using 2-1/2" screws and glue. This completes the headboard.

steps.title: Build the footboard
steps.body: Construct the footboard the same way as the headboard, with the addition of notching the shorter 2x2 cleats on the back so the side-rail cleats can notch in. Predrill the shorter 2x2s if they show signs of splitting.

steps.title: Attach the side rail cleats
steps.body: Attach the side rail cleats inside the footboard and to the headboard legs with 2-1/2" screws. This completes the footboard. Consider painting or staining all the pieces now, before final assembly in the bedroom.

steps.title: Attach the headboard tie-in
steps.body: Attach the headboard tie-in piece between the side-rail cleats.

steps.title: Build and attach the center legs
steps.body: Build the center legs, spacing them about a third of the way down the bed's length — this doesn't need to be exact. Attach the center legs inside the bed at this spacing.

steps.title: Attach the side rails
steps.body: Attach the side rails to the side-rail cleats. Brackets are recommended at the top to secure the side rails to the headboard and footboard.

steps.title: Install the mattress slats
steps.body:
Cut the mattress slats and lay them about 4" apart across the bed. Screw down with 2-1/2" screws.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: King size farmhouse bed with planked headboard and footboard
